documentation: Load processor sequence, their options and other pipeline config from a JSON string.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.pipeline.PipelineManager.run
documentation:
TODO:
The below pseudo code does not work.  The thing that dequeus and locks need to be in one context as you may have
to pop several jobs before finding one that can lock all the input UUIDs.  If the work dispatcher does it, then
it does not know the processors that may write or are read only.  If the work does the above, then how is the
queue weighting and iteration get coordinated across all the workers.

Construct a **VconProcessorIO** object from the vcon_uuids as input to the first VconProcessor
Lock the vcons as needed
run the sequence of **VconProcessor**s passing the constructed **VconPocessorIO** 
object to the first in the sequence and then passing its output to the next and so on for the whole sequence.
Commit the new and changed **Vcon**s from the final **VconProcessor** in the sequence's output

methods.name: py_vcon_server.pipeline.PipelineManager.pipeline_sequence_may_write
documentation: Look up to see if any of the **VconProcessor**s in the pipeline's sequence may modify its input **Vcon**s.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessorIO.is_vcon_modified
documentation:
Get modified state of **Vcon** at given **index**.

Returns: tru/false if the **Vcon** at index is marked
  as modified, meaning needing to be committed.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessorIO.num_vcons
documentation: Return the number of **Vcons** in this **VconProcessorIO** object

methods.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessorIO.get_vcon
documentation: Get the Vcon at index in the form indicated by vcon_type

methods.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessorIO.add_vcon
documentation:
Add the given Vcon to this **VconProcessorIO** object.
It will NOT add the Vcon to storage as the time this 
method is invoked.

If the lock_key is provied, the vCon will be updated in
VconStorage at the end of the pipeline processing,
only if the vCon is modified via the update_vcon
method.

If no lock_key is provided AND the readonly == False,
the Vcon will be added to VconStorage after all of the 
pipeline processing has occurred.  If a vCon with the 
same UUID exist in the VconStorage, prior to the
VconStorage add, the VconStorage add will result in an
error.

returns: index of the added vCon

methods.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessorIO.update_vcon
documentation:
Update an existing vCon in the VconProcessorIO object.
Does not update the Vcon in storage.
The update of the stored Vcon occurs at the end of the pipeline if the Vcon was updated.

Returns: index of updated vCon or None

classes.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessor

methods.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessor.__init__
documentation:
Initialization method used to construct a **VconProcessor**
instance for the **VconProcessorRegistry**.

Parms:
  init_options: VconProcessorInitOptions - options used to
    initialize this instance
  title: str - title or short description of what this
    registered instance of VconProcessor will do in the
    process method.  Should be specific to the options provided.
  description: str - long description of what this instance does
  version: str - version of this class derived from
    **VconProcessor**
  processor_options_class: typing.Type[VconProcessorOptions] -
    The class type of the options input to the **processor** method
    derived from the **VconProcessorOptions**.
  may_modify_vcons: bool - policy of whether this derived **VconProcessor**'s
    **processor* method may modify any of the **Vcon**s in the
    **VconProcessorIO**.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessor.process
documentation:
Abstract method for processing a **Vcon**.  Must
be implemented in derived classes.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessor.version
documentation: Get the module version of the derived **VconProcessor**.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessor.title
documentation: Get the short description or title for the derived **VconProcessor**.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessor.description
documentation: Get the long description for the derived **VconProcessor**.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessor.may_modify_vcons
documentation:
Returns whether the derived **VconProcessor** may
modify any of the input **Vcon**s.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessor.processor_options_class
documentation:
Returns the class type of the expected options
argument to the derived **VconProcessor.process** method.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.processor.VconProcessor.__del__
documentation: Teardown/uninitialization method for the VconProcessor

Abstract base class to all vCon processors.

A vCon Processor generally takes zero or more Vcons as input
and produces some sort of output which may include:

  * A modification of one or more of the input vCons
  * The creation of one or more new Vcons
  * An extraction of data from the input
  * Emmition of a report (e.g. via email or slack)

**VconProcessor**s may be sequenced together (1 or more)
in a **Pipeline**.  A **VconProcessorIO** object is provided as
input to the first **VconProcessor** which outputs a
**VconProcessorIO** that become the input to the next **VconProcessor**
in the **Pipeline** and so on.

The **VconProcessor** contains the method **process** which performs
the work.  It takes a **VconProcessorIO** object as input which contains
the zero or vCon.  The **process** method also takes a
**VconProcessorOptions** object which is where additional input 
parameters are provided as defined by the **VconProcessor**.  The
**processor** method always provides output in the return in
the form of a **VconProcessorIO** object.  Typically this is the same
**VconProcessorIO** that was input with some or no modification.  If
vCon(s) in the input **VconProcessorIO** are not included in the output (if the
**VconProcessorIO** was modified by prior **VconProcessor**s in
the **Pipeline**) any created or modified vCons from the input
will be lost and not saved to the **VconStorage** database.  Care
should be made that this is intensional.

A concrete **VconProcessor** derives from **VconProcessor** and implements
the abstract methods.  If it requires or has optional additional
input parameters, it defines a subclass of the **VconProcessorOptions**
class.  The derived **VconProcessorOptions** class for the derived
**VconProcessor** serves to document the additional input parameters
and helps to validate the input.

A well behaved VconProcessor does not modify the VconStorage
database at all.  Vcons are modified in the **VconProcessorIO** input
and pass on as output.  It is up to the invoker of the **process**
method to decide when to commit the changed to the **VconStorage** database.
For example after all **VconProcessors** in a **Pipeline** sequence
have been processed.  The **VconProcessorIO** keeps track of **Vcon**s
that have been changed to ease the decision of what needs to be commited.

A **VconProcessor** is typically dynamically loaded at startup and gets
registered in the **VconProcessorRegistry**.  A when a concrete 
**VconProcessor** is registered, it is loaded from a given package,
given a unique name and instantiated from the given class name from
that package.  Ths allows serveral instances of a concrete 
**VconProcessor** to be instantiated, each with a unique name and
different set of initialization options.  The class MUST also
implement a static parameter: **initialization_options_class**.
The **initialization_options_class** value MUST be the derived
class of **VconProcessorInitializationOptions** that is used to
validate the options provided to the concrete **VconProcessor**
__init__ method.

modules.name: py_vcon_server.states
documentation:
Interface for server states

The various states that are used include:
  server instances
    host
    port
    pid
    name/id
    start time
    vcon_api_enabled
    admin_api_enabled
    num_workers
    last heartbeat time
    state =  one of: "unknown", "starting_up", "running", "shutting_down"

  pipeline definitions
    input queue name
    vcon: { readonly, readwrite}
    timeout
    array of processor names and options

  pipeline jobs in progress
    server name/id
    start time
    timeout
    queue name
    queue item (type, and info)
Copyright (C) 2023-2024 SIPez LLC.  All rights reserved.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.db.VconStorage.set
documentation: add or update a Vcon in persistent storage

methods.name: py_vcon_server.db.VconStorage.commit

Helper function to save changed **Vcon**s from the
output of a **VconProcessor** or **Pipeline**.

Saves **Vcon**s which have been marked as modified
or new in the given **VconProcessorIO**

methods.name: py_vcon_server.db.VconStorage.get
documentation: Get a Vcon from storage using its UUID as the key

methods.name: py_vcon_server.db.VconStorage.jq_query
documentation:
Apply the given JQ query/transform on the Vcon from storage given its UUID as the key.

Returns: json query/transform in the form of a string

methods.name: py_vcon_server.db.VconStorage.json_path_query
documentation:
Apply the given JsonPath query on the Vcon from storage given its UUID as the key.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.db.VconStorage.delete

Should this be a class or global methods??

modules.name: py_vcon_server.queue
documentation:
Interface for managing job queues and in progress jobs.

DB keys:

"jobid" last job ID used new obtained via INCR
"queues" hash of names of queues
"queue:<queue_name" list of jobs
"inprogress" hash of job ids for in progress jobs
Copyright (C) 2023-2024 SIPez LLC.  All rights reserved.

classes.name: py_vcon_server.queue.EmptyJobQueue
documentation: Raised when trying to pop a job from an empty queue

classes.name: py_vcon_server.queue.QueueDoesNotExist
documentation: Raised when attempting to operate on a queue which does not exist.

classes.name: py_vcon_server.queue.JobDoesNotExist
documentation: Raised when attempting to operate on an in progress job which does not exist.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.queue.JobQueue.get_queue_names
documentation: Get the list of names of all of the existing job queues

methods.name: py_vcon_server.queue.JobQueue.get_queue_jobs
documentation: Get the list of all jobs in the named queue

methods.name: py_vcon_server.queue.JobQueue.get_last_job_id
documentation: Get the id that was used for the last job

methods.name: py_vcon_server.queue.JobQueue.create_new_queue
documentation: Create a new job queue with the given name

methods.name: py_vcon_server.queue.JobQueue.delete_queue
documentation:
Pull all of the jobs off of the named, queue and
remove the queue and its name from the list of queues.

returns: the list of jobs removed from the queue

methods.name: py_vcon_server.queue.JobQueue.pop_queued_job
documentation:
Retrieve the next available job, if one eixst,
from the named queue for the named server,
assign it a job id and add it to the in progress
hash.

Returns: an in progress job object (dict) or None
  keys:
    id: int - unique job id for this job on the given server
    queue: str - name of the queue from which the job was popped
    job: dict - queue job object
    dequeued: float - epoch time UTC when the job was dequeued
    server: str - server_key: "<host>:<port>:<pid>:start_time>" for server
      which will run the job, this is attained from the "/servers" entry 
      point in the admin REST API or from ServerState.server_key()

methods.name: py_vcon_server.queue.JobQueue.get_in_progress_jobs
documentation: Get the list of jobs which are in progress

methods.name: py_vcon_server.queue.JobQueue.requeue_in_progress_job
documentation:
Roll back an incompleted, in progress queue job.

The job is removed from the in progress list and pushed
to the front of the queue from which the job was 
previously popped before being added to the in progress
hash.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.queue.JobQueue.remove_in_progress_job
documentation: Remove the indentified job from the in progress list as completed.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.queue.JobQueue.push_vcon_uuid_queue_job
documentation:
Push a vCon UUID queue job object onto the named queue.

params:
  vcon_uuid: list of one or more vCon UUID strings.
    The UUID must reference a vCon in VconStorage

    The queue gets a queue jobe object which is a json
    dict object with the following keys and values:
      job_type: str = "vcon_uuid"
      vcon_uuid: list[str] vcon_uuids

returns: length of the queue (where the new job is in the queue)

classes.name: py_vcon_server.queue.QueueIterator

methods.name: py_vcon_server.queue.QueueIterator.get_queue_count
documentation: Return the number of queues contifigured (ignoring weight)

methods.name: py_vcon_server.queue.QueueIterator.get_next_queue
documentation: returns the next queue name to use considering weights

methods.name: py_vcon_server.queue.QueueIterator.check_update
documentation:
Check if the configured queues for this server have changed.
Update the snapshot of the queue config and reset the cached
queue sequence.

Class to iterate through the weighted list of job queues configured for the server.

A queue may have a weight (defaults to 1) which indicates how many times the queue
should be used/checked before moving to the next queue.

The iterator keeps a cached copy of the configuration and checks for changes using 
the **check_update** method.

modules.name: py_vcon_server.db.redis.redis_mgr
documentation:
Package to manage Redis connection pool and clients

Setup of redis clients cannot be done globally in each module as it will 
bind to a asyncio loop which may be started and stopped.  In which case
redis will be bound to an old loop which will no longer work

The redis connection pool must be shutdown and restarted when FASTApi does.
Copyright (C) 2023-2024 SIPez LLC.  All rights reserved.

documentation: raised when redis_mgr is not initialized

documentation: Create a redis client pool

methods.name: py_vcon_server.db.redis.redis_mgr.RedisMgr.shutdown_pool
documentation: shutdown the client pool and wait for busy ones to complete

documentation: Log infor about current client pool

methods.name: py_vcon_server.db.redis.redis_mgr.RedisMgr.get_client
documentation: get a redis client from the pool
Interface/wrapper for redis clients and the management of them

documentation: Thrown when Job Scheduler processes fail to start
CONTEXT_METHOD = "fork"
CONTEXT_METHOD = "forkserver"

classes.name: py_vcon_server.job_worker_pool.JobInterface

methods.name: py_vcon_server.job_worker_pool.JobInterface.get_job
documentation: Get the definition of the next job to run. Called in the context of the scheduler/dispatcher process.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.job_worker_pool.JobInterface.do_job
documentation: Function to perform job given job definition.  Called in context of worker process.

methods.name: py_vcon_server.job_worker_pool.JobInterface.job_finished
documentation: handle a successful completion of a job

methods.name: py_vcon_server.job_worker_pool.JobInterface.job_canceled
documentation: handle a cancelled job (only those that have not yet been started)

methods.name: py_vcon_server.job_worker_pool.JobInterface.job_exception
documentation: handle a job which threw an exception and did not complete (including jobs that have been started and then cancelled)

Abstract interface for getting jobs to run and handling state updates after the job is done.
This class must be derived and its abstract methods implemented.  The derived class must be 
picklable.

documentation: Start scheduler and worker processes and feed them jobs

methods.name: py_vcon_server.job_worker_pool.JobSchedulerManager.abort

documentation: Get the number of jobs currently in process by workers
